focus: test-to-claim closure for all six active P1 findings, with stable-ID numeric-confidence refinement of P1-006

findingDetails.title: Post-query generation drift is mislabeled as no-fit in every corpus consumer
findingDetails.evidence: All four consumers retain no-fit for post-query generation drift, and their green concurrent-change tests do not assert the modeled requery-required proof state.
findingDetails.scopeProof: A manifest-restricted search of 21 test files found generation-mismatch coverage but no requery-required assertion; four corpus suites passed with the current behavior.
findingDetails.recommendation: Emit generation-mismatch at all four post-query mismatch branches and assert requery-required while retaining no-source-influence containment.

claimAdjudication.claim: All four corpus consumers suppress their modeled requery-required state by classifying post-query generation drift as no-fit, and the green concurrent-change tests do not assert the required recovery contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Re-read the primary production mismatch branch, searched all 21 manifest-listed tests for generation-mismatch/requery-required/corpus-changing assertions, and reran the four corpus suites. Existing mismatch-plan tests prove no stale hydration, but concurrent-change tests do not require requery-required.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: No-fit could be an intentional safe continuation because the code blocks stale source influence and retains a mismatch warning.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if a governing contract explicitly defines post-query drift as no-fit and executable caller tests prove that no recovery path depends on requery-required.

traceabilityChecks.evidence: Optional verifier tamper tests pass; production open tests cover generation identity only and lack digest binding.
No manifest test covers the playbook; mandatory paths remain removed.
No manifest test compares parent/child state; parent still says Planned.
No manifest test executes README commands; five stale commands remain.
No manifest test covers graph freshness; regeneration owner is outside frozen scope.
Concurrent-change tests pass without asserting requery-required and sometimes explicitly expect no-fit.

noveltyJustification: No new finding was added; one active P1 was materially refined at half weight (2.5) against 30 accumulated active severity weight, yielding 0.0833. The refinement repairs the numeric-confidence gate and adds executable assertion closure without changing stable hash or severity.
edgeCases: Green counts stabilize current behavior but do not prove claim closure.
Generated-metadata regeneration ownership is outside the frozen manifest.

The first manifest-search script had a syntax error and was rerun successfully.
Code Graph unavailable; direct evidence used.

treating absent md-generator dependencies as a product-test failure
